title: Magellan and Elcano — the first circumnavigation

intro.title: Around the World, Westward
intro.body: Ferdinand Magellan sailed from Sanlúcar with five ships and 270 men in September 1519. He was killed at Mactan in April 1521; Juan Sebastián Elcano brought the Victoria home in September 1522 with 18 survivors. Antonio Pigafetta's journal is the eyewitness.

stops.name: Sanlúcar de Barrameda
stops.note: Departure 20 September 1519. The fleet of five ships clears the Guadalquivir bar.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Andalusian river mouth, five caravels and naos under sail, white town on the bank.

stops.name: Strait of Magellan
stops.note: Discovered 21 October 1520. The 38-day passage through the strait at the southern tip of South America opens the Pacific.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Narrow channel between snow-capped headlands, three ships in line with steep cliffs to either side. uninhabited shore, no buildings.

stops.name: Mactan (Philippines)
stops.note: 27 April 1521. Magellan is killed by Lapulapu's warriors in shallow water off the island.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Tropical shore with breaking surf, helmeted Spaniards in knee-deep water, native warriors with spears on the beach. uninhabited shore battle on the beach, no buildings.

stops.name: Tidore (Spice Islands)
stops.note: Victoria and Trinidad reach the Moluccas 8 November 1521. The cloves loaded here pay for the entire expedition.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Volcanic peak above a tropical island, ships taking on sacks of cloves, sultan's pavilion on the strand.
stops._viaComment: Victoria's WESTWARD return (Elcano, 1522): south of the Indonesian arc into the open Indian Ocean, across to round the Cape of Good Hope, then north up the Atlantic to Sanlúcar. These waypoints keep the sea leg over water instead of drawing a straight chord across Asia + Arabia.

stops.name: Sanlúcar (return)
stops.note: Victoria returns 6 September 1522 under Elcano with 18 starved men. They walk barefoot in procession to the cathedral.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Same Andalusian river mouth, one battered ship limping in, ragged men kneeling on the quay.
